title: 3M: Multi-loss, Multi-path and Multi-level Neural Networks for speech recognition
title_zh: 3M：用于语音识别的多损失、多路径和多级神经网络
abstract: Recently, Conformer based CTC/AED model has become a mainstream architecture for ASR. In this paper, based on our prior work, we identify and integrate several approaches to achieve further improvements for ASR tasks, which we denote as multi-loss, multi-path and multi-level, summarized as "3M" model. Specifically, multi-loss refers to the joint CTC/AED loss and multi-path denotes the Mixture-of-Experts(MoE) architecture which can effectively increase the model capacity without remarkably increasing computation cost. Multi-level means that we introduce auxiliary loss at multiple level of a deep model to help training. We evaluate our proposed method on the public WenetSpeech dataset and experimental results show that the proposed method provides 12.2%-17.6% relative CER improvement over the baseline model trained by Wenet toolkit. On our large scale dataset of 150k hours corpus, the 3M model has also shown obvious superiority over the baseline Conformer model. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/tencent-ailab/3m-asr.
summary: Recently, Conformer based CTC/AED model has become a mainstream architecture for ASR. In this paper, based on our prior work, we identify and integrate several approaches to achieve further improvements for ASR tasks, which we denote as multi-loss, multi-path and multi-level, summarized as "3M" model. Specifically, multi-loss refers to the joint CTC/AED loss and multi-path denotes the Mixture-of-Experts(MoE) architecture which can effectively increase the model capacity without remarkably increasing computation cost. Multi-level means that we introduce auxiliary loss at multiple level of a deep model to help training. We evaluate our proposed method on the public WenetSpeech dataset and experimental results show that the proposed method provides 12.2%-17.6% relative CER improvement over the baseline model trained by Wenet toolkit. On our large scale dataset of 150k hours corpus, the 3M model has also shown obvious superiority over the baseline Conformer model. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/tencent-ailab/3m-asr.
abstract_zh: 近年来，基于Conformer的CTC/AED模型已成为ASR的主流架构。在本文中，基于我们之前的工作，我们确定并整合了几种方法来实现 ASR 任务的进一步改进，我们将其表示为多损失、多路径和多级别，概括为“3M”模型。具体来说，多损失是指联合CTC/AED损失，多路径表示专家混合（MoE）架构，它可以在不显着增加计算成本的情况下有效增加模型容量。多级意味着我们在深度模型的多个级别引入辅助损失来帮助训练。我们在公共 WenetSpeech 数据集上评估了我们提出的方法，实验结果表明，与 Wenet 工具包训练的基线模型相比，该方法提供了 12.2%-17.6% 的相对 CER 改进。在我们 15 万小时语料库的大规模数据集上，3M 模型也表现出了比基线 Conformer 模型明显的优越性。代码可在 https://github.com/tencent-ailab/3m-asr 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合多损失、多路径和多级结构的3M模型，用于提升语音识别任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 尽管Conformer基于CTC/AED模型已成为ASR主流架构，但作者旨在通过集成多种改进方法进一步提升模型效果。
AI.method: 3M模型包含三个核心组件：联合CTC/AED多损失函数、基于MoE的多路径架构以及深度网络多级辅助损失机制。
AI.result: 在WenetSpeech数据集上相对CER提升12.2%-17.6%，在150k小时大规模数据集上也显著优于基线Conformer模型。
AI.conclusion: 3M模型通过多维度优化有效提升了语音识别性能，且代码已开源供研究使用。

ai_content.motivation: 尽管Conformer基于CTC/AED模型已成为ASR主流架构，但作者旨在通过集成多种改进方法进一步提升模型效果。

ai_content.result: 在WenetSpeech数据集上相对CER提升12.2%-17.6%，在150k小时大规模数据集上也显著优于基线Conformer模型。
ai_content.conclusion: 3M模型通过多维度优化有效提升了语音识别性能，且代码已开源供研究使用。

search_text: 3M: Multi-loss, Multi-path and Multi-level Neural Networks for speech recognition 3M：用于语音识别的多损失、多路径和多级神经网络 Recently, Conformer based CTC/AED model has become a mainstream architecture for ASR. In this paper, based on our prior work, we identify and integrate several approaches to achieve further improvements for ASR tasks, which we denote as multi-loss, multi-path and multi-level, summarized as "3M" model. Specifically, multi-loss refers to the joint CTC/AED loss and multi-path denotes the Mixture-of-Experts(MoE) architecture which can effectively increase the model capacity without remarkably increasing computation cost. Multi-level means that we introduce auxiliary loss at multiple level of a deep model to help training. We evaluate our proposed method on the public WenetSpeech dataset and experimental results show that the proposed method provides 12.2%-17.6% relative CER improvement over the baseline model trained by Wenet toolkit. On our large scale dataset of 150k hours corpus, the 3M model has also shown obvious superiority over the baseline Conformer model. title: FedCos: A Scene-adaptive Federated Optimization Enhancement for Performance Improvement
title_zh: FedCos：场景自适应联合优化增强性能提升
abstract: As an emerging technology, federated learning (FL) involves training machine learning models over distributed edge devices, which attracts sustained attention and has been extensively studied. However, the heterogeneity of client data severely degrades the performance of FL compared with that in centralized training. It causes the locally trained models of clients to move in different directions. On the one hand, it slows down or even stalls the global updates, leading to inefficient communication. On the other hand, it enlarges the distances between local models, resulting in an aggregated global model with poor performance. Fortunately, these shortcomings can be mitigated by reducing the angle between the directions that local models move in. Based on this fact, we propose FedCos, which reduces the directional inconsistency of local models by introducing a cosine-similarity penalty. It promotes the local model iterations towards an auxiliary global direction. Moreover, our approach is auto-adapt to various non-IID settings without an elaborate selection of hyperparameters. The experimental results show that FedCos outperforms the well-known baselines and can enhance them under a variety of FL scenes, including varying degrees of data heterogeneity, different number of participants, and cross-silo and cross-device settings. Besides, FedCos improves communication efficiency by 2 to 5 times. With the help of FedCos, multiple FL methods require significantly fewer communication rounds than before to obtain a model with comparable performance.
summary: As an emerging technology, federated learning (FL) involves training machine learning models over distributed edge devices, which attracts sustained attention and has been extensively studied. However, the heterogeneity of client data severely degrades the performance of FL compared with that in centralized training. It causes the locally trained models of clients to move in different directions. On the one hand, it slows down or even stalls the global updates, leading to inefficient communication. On the other hand, it enlarges the distances between local models, resulting in an aggregated global model with poor performance. Fortunately, these shortcomings can be mitigated by reducing the angle between the directions that local models move in. Based on this fact, we propose FedCos, which reduces the directional inconsistency of local models by introducing a cosine-similarity penalty. It promotes the local model iterations towards an auxiliary global direction. Moreover, our approach is auto-adapt to various non-IID settings without an elaborate selection of hyperparameters. The experimental results show that FedCos outperforms the well-known baselines and can enhance them under a variety of FL scenes, including varying degrees of data heterogeneity, different number of participants, and cross-silo and cross-device settings. Besides, FedCos improves communication efficiency by 2 to 5 times. With the help of FedCos, multiple FL methods require significantly fewer communication rounds than before to obtain a model with comparable performance.
abstract_zh: 作为一项新兴技术，联邦学习（FL）涉及在分布式边缘设备上训练机器学习模型，受到持续关注并得到广泛研究。然而，与集中训练相比，客户端数据的异构性严重降低了 FL 的性能。它导致本地训练的客户模型朝不同的方向移动。一方面，它减慢甚至停止全局更新，导致通信效率低下。另一方面，它扩大了局部模型之间的距离，导致聚合的全局模型性能较差。幸运的是，这些缺点可以通过减小局部模型移动方向之间的角度来缓解。基于这一事实，我们提出了 FedCos，它通过引入余弦相似性惩罚来减少局部模型的方向不一致。它促进局部模型迭代向辅助全局方向发展。此外，我们的方法可以自动适应各种非独立同分布设置，无需精心选择超参数。实验结果表明，FedCos 的性能优于众所周知的基线，并且可以在各种 FL 场景下增强它们，包括不同程度的数据异构性、不同数量的参与者以及跨孤岛和跨设备设置。此外，FedCos 将通信效率提高了 2 至 5 倍。在 FedCo 的帮助下，多种 FL 方法需要比以前少得多的通信次数才能获得性能相当的模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FedCos方法，通过引入余弦相似度惩罚机制，提升联邦学习在异构数据场景下的性能。
AI.motivation: 联邦学习中客户端数据异构性导致本地模型更新方向不一致，造成全局模型收敛效率低、性能差的问题。
AI.method: 设计基于余弦相似度的惩罚项，强制本地模型迭代方向与辅助全局方向对齐，无需手动调整超参数即可适应不同非独立同分布场景。
AI.result: FedCos在多种联邦学习场景下优于现有基线方法，通信效率提升2-5倍，显著减少达到目标性能所需的通信轮次。
AI.conclusion: FedCos通过方向对齐策略有效缓解数据异构性影响，在保持模型性能的同时大幅提升联邦学习的通信效率和适应性。

search_text: FedCos: A Scene-adaptive Federated Optimization Enhancement for Performance Improvement FedCos：场景自适应联合优化增强性能提升 As an emerging technology, federated learning (FL) involves training machine learning models over distributed edge devices, which attracts sustained attention and has been extensively studied. However, the heterogeneity of client data severely degrades the performance of FL compared with that in centralized training. It causes the locally trained models of clients to move in different directions. On the one hand, it slows down or even stalls the global updates, leading to inefficient communication. On the other hand, it enlarges the distances between local models, resulting in an aggregated global model with poor performance. Fortunately, these shortcomings can be mitigated by reducing the angle between the directions that local models move in. Based on this fact, we propose FedCos, which reduces the directional inconsistency of local models by introducing a cosine-similarity penalty. It promotes the local model iterations towards an auxiliary global direction. Moreover, our approach is auto-adapt to various non-IID settings without an elaborate selection of hyperparameters. The experimental results show that FedCos outperforms the well-known baselines and can enhance them under a variety of FL scenes, including varying degrees of data heterogeneity, different number of participants, and cross-silo and cross-device settings. Besides, FedCos improves communication efficiency by 2 to 5 times. With the help of FedCos, multiple FL methods require significantly fewer communication rounds than before to obtain a model with comparable performance. 作为一项新兴技术，联邦学习（FL）涉及在分布式边缘设备上训练机器学习模型，受到持续关注并得到广泛研究。然而，与集中训练相比，客户端数据的异构性严重降低了 FL 的性能。它导致本地训练的客户模型朝不同的方向移动。一方面，它减慢甚至停止全局更新，导致通信效率低下。另一方面，它扩大了局部模型之间的距离，导致聚合的全局模型性能较差。幸运的是，这些缺点可以通过减小局部模型移动方向之间的角度来缓解。基于这一事实，我们提出了 FedCos，它通过引入余弦相似性惩罚来减少局部模型的方向不一致。它促进局部模型迭代向辅助全局方向发展。此外，我们的方法可以自动适应各种非独立同分布设置，无需精心选择超参数。实验结果表明，FedCos 的性能优于众所周知的基线，并且可以在各种 FL 场景下增强它们，包括不同程度的数据异构性、不同数量的参与者以及跨孤岛和跨设备设置。此外，FedCos 将通信效率提高了 2 至 5 倍。在 FedCo 的帮助下，多种 FL 方法需要比以前少得多的通信次数才能获得性能相当的模型。 本文提出FedCos方法，通过引入余弦相似度惩罚机制，提升联邦学习在异构数据场景下的性能。 Hao Zhang,Tingting Wu,Siyao Cheng,Jie Liu cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC

authors_text: Zheng Chen,Ziwei Yang,Lingwei Zhu,Wei Chen,Toshiyo Tamura,Naoaki Ono,MD Altaf-Ul-Amin,Shigehiko Kanaya,Ming Huang
title: Automated Sleep Staging via Parallel Frequency-Cut Attention
title_zh: 通过并行频率削减注意力自动睡眠分期
abstract: This paper proposes a novel framework for automatically capturing the time-frequency nature of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals of human sleep based on the authoritative sleep medicine guidance. The framework consists of two parts: the first part extracts informative features by partitioning the input EEG spectrograms into a sequence of time-frequency patches. The second part is constituted by an attention-based architecture to efficiently search for the correlation between partitioned time-frequency patches and defining factors of sleep stages in parallel. The proposed pipeline is validated on the Sleep Heart Health Study dataset with new state-of-the-art results for the stages wake, N2, and N3, obtaining respective F1 scores of 0.93, 0.88, and 0.87, with only EEG signals used. The proposed method also has a high inter-rater reliability of 0.80 kappa. We also visualize the correspondence between sleep staging decisions and features extracted by the proposed method, providing strong interpretability for our model.
summary: This paper proposes a novel framework for automatically capturing the time-frequency nature of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals of human sleep based on the authoritative sleep medicine guidance. The framework consists of two parts: the first part extracts informative features by partitioning the input EEG spectrograms into a sequence of time-frequency patches. The second part is constituted by an attention-based architecture to efficiently search for the correlation between partitioned time-frequency patches and defining factors of sleep stages in parallel. The proposed pipeline is validated on the Sleep Heart Health Study dataset with new state-of-the-art results for the stages wake, N2, and N3, obtaining respective F1 scores of 0.93, 0.88, and 0.87, with only EEG signals used. The proposed method also has a high inter-rater reliability of 0.80 kappa. We also visualize the correspondence between sleep staging decisions and features extracted by the proposed method, providing strong interpretability for our model.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于权威睡眠医学指南自动捕获人类睡眠脑电图（EEG）信号时频特性的新颖框架。该框架由两部分组成：第一部分通过将输入脑电图频谱图划分为一系列时频块来提取信息特征。第二部分由基于注意力的架构构成，可有效地并行搜索分区时频补丁与睡眠阶段定义因素之间的相关性。所提出的流程在睡眠心脏健康研究数据集上进行了验证，并在唤醒、N2 和 N3 阶段获得了最新的最新结果，仅使用 EEG 信号，分别获得了 0.93、0.88 和 0.87 的 F1 分数。所提出的方法还具有 0.80 kappa 的高评估者间可靠性。我们还可视化了睡眠分期决策与所提出的方法提取的特征之间的对应关系，为我们的模型提供了强大的可解释性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于平行频率切割注意力机制的自动睡眠分期框架，通过分析脑电图（EEG）信号的时间频率特征实现睡眠阶段分类。
AI.motivation: 睡眠分期是睡眠医学的重要环节，传统方法依赖人工标注效率低且主观性强。现有算法在特征提取与阶段定义关联性建模方面存在不足，需提升准确率与可解释性。
AI.method: 框架分两部分：首先将EEG频谱图分割为时间-频率块提取特征；其次通过注意力网络并行搜索各特征块与睡眠阶段定义因素的相关性，实现高效分类。
AI.result: 在Sleep Heart Health Study数据集上，仅使用EEG信号取得Wake/N2/N3阶段F1分数分别为0.93/0.88/0.87，且具有0.80的高组间一致性（kappa值），并通过可视化增强模型可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在睡眠分期任务中达到新SOTA水平，通过平行注意力机制有效捕捉EEG时间-频率特征，同时提供可视化解释，为睡眠障碍诊断提供可靠工具。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于平行频率切割注意力机制的自动睡眠分期框架，通过分析脑电图（EEG）信号的时间频率特征实现睡眠阶段分类。
ai_content.motivation: 睡眠分期是睡眠医学的重要环节，传统方法依赖人工标注效率低且主观性强。现有算法在特征提取与阶段定义关联性建模方面存在不足，需提升准确率与可解释性。
ai_content.method: 框架分两部分：首先将EEG频谱图分割为时间-频率块提取特征；其次通过注意力网络并行搜索各特征块与睡眠阶段定义因素的相关性，实现高效分类。
ai_content.result: 在Sleep Heart Health Study数据集上，仅使用EEG信号取得Wake/N2/N3阶段F1分数分别为0.93/0.88/0.87，且具有0.80的高组间一致性（kappa值），并通过可视化增强模型可解释性。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法在睡眠分期任务中达到新SOTA水平，通过平行注意力机制有效捕捉EEG时间-频率特征，同时提供可视化解释，为睡眠障碍诊断提供可靠工具。

search_text: Automated Sleep Staging via Parallel Frequency-Cut Attention 通过并行频率削减注意力自动睡眠分期 This paper proposes a novel framework for automatically capturing the time-frequency nature of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals of human sleep based on the authoritative sleep medicine guidance. The framework consists of two parts: the first part extracts informative features by partitioning the input EEG spectrograms into a sequence of time-frequency patches. The second part is constituted by an attention-based architecture to efficiently search for the correlation between partitioned time-frequency patches and defining factors of sleep stages in parallel. The proposed pipeline is validated on the Sleep Heart Health Study dataset with new state-of-the-art results for the stages wake, N2, and N3, obtaining respective F1 scores of 0.93, 0.88, and 0.87, with only EEG signals used. The proposed method also has a high inter-rater reliability of 0.80 kappa. We also visualize the correspondence between sleep staging decisions and features extracted by the proposed method, providing strong interpretability for our model. 本文提出了一种基于权威睡眠医学指南自动捕获人类睡眠脑电图（EEG）信号时频特性的新颖框架。该框架由两部分组成：第一部分通过将输入脑电图频谱图划分为一系列时频块来提取信息特征。第二部分由基于注意力的架构构成，可有效地并行搜索分区时频补丁与睡眠阶段定义因素之间的相关性。所提出的流程在睡眠心脏健康研究数据集上进行了验证，并在唤醒、N2 和 N3 阶段获得了最新的最新结果，仅使用 EEG 信号，分别获得了 0.93、0.88 和 0.87 的 F1 分数。所提出的方法还具有 0.80 kappa 的高评估者间可靠性。我们还可视化了睡眠分期决策与所提出的方法提取的特征之间的对应关系，为我们的模型提供了强大的可解释性。 本文提出一种基于平行频率切割注意力机制的自动睡眠分期框架，通过分析脑电图（EEG）信号的时间频率特征实现睡眠阶段分类。 Zheng Chen,Ziwei Yang,Lingwei Zhu,Wei Chen,Toshiyo Tamura,Naoaki Ono,MD Altaf-Ul-Amin,Shigehiko Kanaya,Ming Huang cs.LG,cs.AI,eess.SP
